about: routes-manifest.json — THE ONE LIST OF ROOMS (GATE-EFFICIENCY-PLAN G3, recommendation R8).
Every room this app serves, named once. Five separate hand-kept lists used to answer the
question 'what rooms are there?' — a11y.spec.ts's ROUTES, hardening.spec.ts's ROUTES and
SEEDED_ROUTES, vrt.spec.ts's SEEDED_ROOMS, check-nav.mjs's PRODUCT_ROUTES, and
check-bundles.mjs's BASELINE_KB — and they disagreed with each other and with the app.
That is not a tidiness problem. It is how /news shipped in N5, the densest room in the app,
and went through TWO tagged phases without a single sweep looking at it. Nothing failed,
because nothing was ever asked to look. Five lists is five chances to forget, and forgetting
is silent in all five.
So: one list, and lib/routes-manifest.test.ts stands over it. That test walks app/app/**/page.tsx,
derives the route each page actually serves, and fails if the filesystem and this file disagree
in EITHER direction — a new room nothing measures, or an entry for a room that is gone (the
sweeps would 'pass' on a deleted room forever, because a 404 page has no controls under 44px).
It is JSON, not TypeScript, for one reason: the .mjs budget scripts, the TypeScript e2e specs
and vitest all have to read it, and JSON is the only format all three read without ceremony.
JSON has no comments, so every entry carries its argument in its own `note` field. An entry
with no note is an entry nobody has had to defend.
THE FIELDS:
path      The concrete URL a tool can actually open. For a dynamic family this is the
canonical instance the specs already use — /news/[cluster] cannot be fetched, and a
sweep pointed at a cluster id that is not seeded measures the 404 page and passes.
family    The route as the App Router sees it, brackets and all. This is the key the
completeness test matches against the filesystem, and the key check-bundles.mjs
baselines against.
seeded    True when the URL only EXISTS if the database carries the synthetic morning. Ask
for an unseeded story cluster and the route calls notFound(), so the sweep measures
an error page and reports the room clean (N7). These routes are split out and
skipped unless MSM_SEEDED=1 — they are never quietly swept against a missing page.
sweeps    Which whole-app rules walk this room: touch (44px targets), scroll (nothing goes
sideways), axe (WCAG serious/critical, both themes). Empty means no sweep goes here,
and the note has to say why.
navBudget gated   — check-nav.mjs times it and B2 fails if the warm median misses.
control — timed and reported, never gated (a static page, there for comparison).
pending — probed before it is built. A 404 is the fastest response a server can
give, so an unbuilt route must never be scored as a fast one. Empty today:
F3 shipped the last pending route. The mechanism stays for the next one.
none    — not probed at all, and the note says why.
vrtRoom   The room slug vrt.spec.ts's generated room loop shoots (light + dark, every
viewport). Null means the room has no GENERATED shot — it may still have bespoke
ones written by hand in the spec, and where it does, the note says so.
NOT IN THIS FILE, deliberately: /login (the wall, not a room behind it) and /styleguide (the
living spec — a developer surface). Both are argued in routes-manifest.test.ts's exemption list,
and the tools that still need them name them explicitly, in one place each, with a reason.
There is no per-route `wide` flag. The wide viewport (1536) is scoped by SPEC FILE in
playwright.config.ts — the `wide` project runs vrt and hardening and nothing else — so a
per-route flag would have no consumer. A field nobody reads is a measurement that is not being
taken, wearing a measurement's clothes. See QUESTIONS-FOR-BISHANT.md, Q-G3-1.

routes.note: The Desk. Carries the pipeline strip, which grades freshness against the READER's clock — so its baseline is shot under a pinned clock (e2e/seeded-clock.ts) and asserts the strip is FRESH before photographing it.

The Front Page (N5). The room this whole manifest exists because of: it shipped unmeasured and stayed unmeasured for two tagged phases. It is also the densest room in the app for the touch rule — two horizontally scrolling chip rows, a pagination control and a card grid of links.

One story. SEEDED: ask for a cluster id the database does not have and the route calls notFound(), which answers HTTP 200 with the 404 page in the body — so an unseeded sweep measures an error page and reports the story page clean. Verified: this happened.

The match table (F3) — the app's one DataTable, with the recipe card, the named default order and the pagination footer. Not seeded: /scans/[preset] declares dynamicParams=false over a closed set of five, so the room renders its empty state without the seed rather than 404ing.

The instrument room. The sweeps and the nav budget open SPY, which needs no seed. Its pixel baselines are shot BESPOKE in vrt.spec.ts at /ticker/AAPL instead (snapshots ticker-light/ticker-dark), because the Range Ladder only renders with seeded vol bands behind it — hence vrtRoom is null here while the room does have baselines.

The paper ledger. Its fixture (prisma/fixtures/paper.mjs) is RELATIVE to the run, and had to be: it carried absolute dates until N7, and the baseline expired 28 minutes after the run that last certified it. That is the fuse drift rule 21 now hunts.
The ledger of what fired and what it did — insert-only, misses public.

The control room (N6): five action rows, ten states, the P-2 notice. The app's ONE writer room, so it is the one route allowed force-dynamic (argued in check-routes.mjs, check-drift rule 13 and its own page.tsx). It answers in ~430-510ms every sample, every one a cache MISS — correct, not a regression, which is why B2 is in report mode.

The Academy index. Its VRT shot photographs lesson progress, so the suite clears lesson_progress and busts the ISR cache before shooting — the baselines were otherwise born in a different world than the one they are checked in.
CONTROL for the nav budget: a fully static page, timed and reported beside the product rooms as the fast path they are all measured against — never gated, because it is the yardstick.

One lesson, standing for the lesson family in the nav budget. NOT swept and NOT pixel-locked, and that is a real gap rather than a decision: a lesson is prose in the Academy's own frame, and the frame is already swept at /academy — but no sweep has ever measured a lesson's own controls (the glossary popovers are here). Logged as Q-G3-2; the manifest is now the place to close it.

The page of last resort, served by the service worker when a navigation fails and nothing is cached. Not swept and not probed BY CONSTRUCTION: every sweep begins by signing in and navigating, which is exactly the thing that must have failed for a reader to be here, and timing it over a working network would measure a page nobody reaches that way. e2e/offline.spec.ts owns it, with the network actually turned off.
